expectations.text: Mentions KaTeX as the validation library
expectations.evidence: Response recommends KaTeX as the core validation library.

expectations.text: Provides an install command (bun add -g katex or npm install -g katex)
expectations.evidence: Response provides npm install katex instructions.

expectations.text: Provides or references a Node.js/JavaScript script that validates all three block types
expectations.evidence: Response provides a complete self-contained Node.js script handling all three block types.

expectations.text: Mentions that the solution exits with code 1 on errors (CI-friendly)
expectations.evidence: Response mentions 'exits with code 1' and provides a GitHub Actions workflow example.

expectations.text: Notes the important caveat that KaTeX passing does NOT guarantee GitHub renders correctly
expectations.evidence: Response never mentions the GitHub pre-processor issue. Users will be confused when equations validate locally but fail on GitHub.
